title: Mexican Pickled Onions
summary: These cebolla encurtidas, or red pickled onions, are an easy but important dish for any Mexican party. Store a batch in the fridge and use them to garnish tacos, tostadas or any Mexican antojito.

method.description: Peel and cut the onion into thin strips then place in a mixing bowl.
Add the salt to the onions and mix to combine. Set aside for around 10 mins (this will remove some of the moisture of the onion).
Rinse the onions under cold water to get rid of the salt, then pat the onions dry.
Add the vinegar, water, oregano, peppercorns and bay leaf to the bowl with the onions. Mix well and leave to pickle for a few hours.
Serve as garnish for tacos or any Mexican antojito.

chefs.name: Karla Zazueta
chefs.summary: Karla Zazueta is an incredible Mexican cook, food writer and cookery teacher. She teaches her Mexican traditions, recipes and techniques through cooking classes at her Mexican Food Memories cooking school. She also hosts Mexican supper clubs at her family home. Her recipes have been featured in The Guardian, BBC Food, NatGeo Food Magazine and many more. She's also just released her first cookbook, Norteña, where she shares the story of her family recipes and northern Mexico.
